title: Any() should be preferred to test for emptiness
description: When you call Any(), it clearly communicates the code’s intention, which is to check if the collection is empty. Using Count() == 0 for this purpose is less direct and makes the code slightly more complex. However, there are some cases where special attention should be paid: if the collection is an EntityFramework or other ORM query, calling Count() will cause executing a potentially massive SQL query and could put a large overhead on the application database.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Use Any() to test for emptiness instead of Count() comparisons.
